desc:
This is a sharply-slanted shaft whose floor is covered with loose scree. It does
not appear to be a natural formation, but it certainly wasn't made as a
passageway - at least not for feet such as yours. As you scramble down here you
dislodge numerous rocks, which tumble below you with a series of small, soft
echoes.
name: A Crumbling Shaft

desc:
This is a small tunnel, only five or six feet tall at the highest. You can see
from the marks on the walls that it was hewn by picks from the hard grey stone
surrounding you. The passageway is half filled with broken shards of rock, and
the western end has completely collapsed. The passage slopes up to the east,
where it also seems to expand.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
The passageway gets progressively larger as it ascends to the east, and here it
is large enough for a group of three or four to walk abreast. The walls are
rough and undecorated, but the floor is completely smooth and covered with a
thin coat of a viscous, sulfurous slime.
name: Sloping Corridor

desc:
The large tunnel here slopes up to the east, where it gets taller and broader,
and finally opens up into a spacious cavern. The floors and walls here are
unadorned, but smooth and well-proportioned. They might once have been pleasant
to walk upon, but are now covered with an acrid green slime which makes movement
here a bit treacherous.
name: Large Corridor

desc:
A large domed chamber stretches off to the south. Cold rock is hard and savage,
but here it has been crafted to a gentle, welcoming form. There are no edges
here, no shadows; each facet of this place flows gently into another, drawing
your eye slowly from floor to ceiling and wall to wall. A faint fresh breeze
blows from the south.
name: Handsome Cavern

desc:
This elegant cavern adjoins a smaller chamber to the east. The arch of the
entranceway is only half as tall as the dome above you, but their lines meet
gently and form one graceful whole. The rock surrounding you has been carved and
polished with such skill that even now it stands serenely unaffected by the
passage of time. In daytime a diffuse light filters in through the opening to
the east and through several shafts in the walls. The walls are unornamented,
but the remnants of a mosaic around the edges of the floor can still be seen. A
thin patina of slime makes the floor a bit slippery.
name: Handsome Cavern

desc:
The east end of this corridor merges seamlessly into a tall and
well-proportioned cavern. As it extends to the west it descends noticeably, as
well as slowly shrinking. This appears to have been an important passageway;
unlike other corridors the walls here were once decorated, and dim remnants of
frescoes are still visible. Ruined statues of Dwarven-lords line the walls, grim
reminders of the transience of glory.
name: Large Corridor

desc:
Here the smooth floor of the cavern meets a wide road leading eastward. The way
is wide and the arched ceiling rises high above, and large wagons or great
companies of men could no doubt pass through unhindered. On the walls are carved
broad scenes which, though weather-beaten, are clearly of a large host of
Dwarves assembled in some company. On either side, near the walls, the floor
once bore a mosaic; it has been all but destroyed by greedy fingers or lashing
winds.
name: Grand Entranceway

desc:
The wind whistles through your hair, refreshing you after the time spent within
the mountain. The cold air around you is invigorating, bracing, so fresh and
clear you feel you could live here forever on it alone. Inexorable gravity,
however, disagrees, and you plummet hundreds of feet down to the remnants of the
road below, and to a particularly gruesome end.
name: Falling down a Cliff

desc:
Slowly the corridor delves its way into the mountain. You begin to feel a slight
increase in heat. The tunnel here is perfectly flat and smooth, and covered with
a thin coat of mire, as if polished over decades by some hideous underground
army.
name: Sloping Corridor

desc:
Deeper and deeper the passage makes its way, slowly bending here to the south.
It gets hotter and hotter as you descend to the very root of the great mountain,
and you seem to detect a faint red glow around the bend. The slime which covers
much of these caverns continues here, if anything thickening as you go down.
Wisps of fog flit across your face.
name: Sloping Corridor

desc:
Whatever it is that warms there caves must be quite close now; you can feel the
heat radiating from the south. Light clouds of steam surround you, and an acrid
reek assails your nostrils. Just south of here the tunnel opens into an enormous
cavern, from which an ominous red glow can be seen.
name: Sloping Corridor

desc:
This shaft runs vertically for many feet; without the ladder running along its
west side you would surely drop straight down to the bottom. As you descend it
grows cooler, and a clammy dew settles on your skin. Finally the ladder ends,
and with your feet you can feel no ledges or handholds further down. The only
way to continue would be a blind leap of faith to the bottom of the shaft,
however far down that may be.
name: Ladder to a Pit

desc:
With no ladder on which to climb, you hurtle downwards through the dense dark
air. The view remains unchanged as you fall, except for occasional corridors
branching off from this shaft. As you gather speed the blood rushes to your
head, your sight reddens and dims, and your consciousness slowly slips away. You
will never know just where is it you finally land.
name: Into the Depths

desc:
You are at the entrance to an enormous cavern, carved deep into the living rock.
This must once have been an important junction, as many passages open up along
the walls. All are blocked now; the long toll of history has effortlessly undone
what mortal hands struggled so hard to build. Or was the destruction purposeful?
The cavern extends beyond the limits of your sight, but in a far off corner you
can spy a faint red glow. The air is hotter in that direction.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
The western wall of this cavern slowly curves around to the east, where there is
definitely a flickering hot red light. No sunshine from above could ever reach
down here, through the curving corridors and cramped mineshafts, to the very
roots of this great mountain. And yet, the eastern air, through veils of mist,
glows red.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
This seems to be the source of the heat, and the fog, and the muck which fouls
these caves. Here, safe from daylight and stinging swords, far from meddling
eyes, some great beast has made its lair.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
The northern wall of the cavern gently bends to the south. Various corridors
branch off, but none progresses more than a few feet; all are collapsed,
rubble-filled. The slime seems thicker here, and the stench more nauseating,
than to the west. To the south, encased here with you, deep below the earth, you
see a crimson glow, and hear a raspy, gurgling sound, like a teapot boiling on
the stove. A huge boulder to the east seems to be blocking some kind of
entrance.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
A vile stench lies over this room, where the dragon seems to have spent much of
its time. The cave is smaller than the one to the west, but still large enough
to hold the dragon.
name: Back Cave

desc:
A mineshaft extends from the cavern like a small tentacle reaching outwards.
From the cavern comes a fierce red heat and waves of steam. Near the entrance a
ladder extends down into a pit. The mineshaft continues only a short while
before your way is blocked by mounds of blasted rock.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
Slowly you climb upon a ladder leading into the inky deepness. You feel a dim
heat radiate at you from above, and a dim fog drifts down around you, making
your hands perilously slippery. Above you see a red glow; below you all is
black.
name: Descent into Darkness

desc:
The passageway here gets progressively smaller as it descends to the west, and
here it is large enough for a group of three or four to walk abreast. The walls
are rough and undecorated, but the floor is completely smooth, and, because of
the slime which covers it, rather slippery.
name: Sloping Corridor

desc:
The large tunnel here slopes down to the west, where it gets steadily smaller
and rougher. The floors and walls here are unadorned, but smooth and well
proportioned. They might once have been pleasant to walk through, but are now
covered with an acrid green slime which has, in many places, hardened into dry
flakes. To the north is a large opening into a spacious cavern.
name: Large Corridor

desc:
A pile of debris blocks the way, and you can go no further. Uncountable tons of
stone surround you. You are deep within the mountain here, far from light and
sky, far from the sun which Arien pulls endlessly above the distant heights of
Middle-earth.
name: Tunnel's End

desc:
Slowly rising, the tunnel follows the grain of the rock. The damp walls sparkle
and glisten around you, reflecting the deep reds and burnt oranges which streak
them. The air here is musty and stale, but cool and odourless, and not
altogether unpleasant.
name: A Mined-Out Vein

desc:
This shaft is now several hundred feet below its top, and still it continues
down. It is cooler here than it was above, and the rough-hewn walls surround you
like a tomb. To the north a passage extends, slanting upwards. Some ways below
the tunnel fills with water, and you can go no further down without submerging
yourself.
name: Under the Mountain

desc:
You are in a small tube, perhaps ten feet high and 10 across, which is filled
with water so icy cold that you have lost all bodily sensation. The water flows
slowly to the west.
name: Icy Blackness

desc:
The tube steadily contracts as it goes west. Here it is no more than 6 feet
high, and thin enough that you can touch both walls at the same time. It is
still entirely filled with water, which is flowing ever faster to the west.
name: Icy Blackness

desc:
The frigid water hurls you along to the west, propelling you through a narrow
cleft barely big enough to hold you. You are dashed against the walls, and there
is no hope of returning to the east. All you can do is hold your breath and hope
against hope that you come out of this alive.
name: Icy Blackness

desc:
The corridor which started out, far above and to the east, with giantish
proportions, has shrunk here to a more Dwarven stature. It is quite rough now;
though the floor is still comfortable to walk upon, the walls and ceilings are
scarred as if by picks, and scattered rocks litter the floor. The slime which
lines the floors to the east is absent here.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
This peak looks much the same as all the others you've climbed. The view is also
much the same. The only thing really of any interest is the peak to the
southwest. Higher than most of the others around here, it stands almost as a
proud warrior of stone. Unfortunately, it appears that the slopes of it are much
too steep to climb.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Here there is a small hollow in the mountains, affording some protection from
the snow and the constant, biting winds. The mountain walls around you are grey
and ragged, and various rocks and protuberances look as if they could come
crashing down at any time. Indeed, the ground around you contains the jagged
remnants of many boulders which have done just that.
name: A Small Depression

desc:
More and more, you dwell on the horror stories of large stone giants that wander
deep in these mountains. The mountain peaks continue north and east of here.
From your location low in the mountains, not much is visible here except for the
endless ranges of stone and rock. With the trail lost, you might never find your
way out of the mountains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
As you pass by the never ending slopes and peaks of the mountains, you begin to
lose hope of ever finding your way back to the trail you lost. While the
mountains continue in their uninteresting fashion in all directions, you find
that you are only able to climb up to a close mountain peak, or north, to more
of the same mountainous terrain.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Ahead of you, a towering mountain rises steadily forward. Its peak, shrouded in
wispy plumes of fog and mist readily fit into the monotonous scene, yet a single
spur races towards you from which a thin curl of steam can most evidently be
seen, causing your eye to pay attention to this behemoth mountain.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Like the tail of a huge scorpion ready to strike, the towering mountain looms
ahead. Its spur, reaches towards you guarding the higher mountain behind it. Up
ahead, the ground increases in pitch, making you bite into the ground firmly.
name: Near the Eastern Spur

desc:
The ground pitches violently upwards here, as the mountain's spur, like a thick
muscular root, digs into the rocky ground and shoots upwards. The thin plume of
steam can be seen coming from directly ahead, no doubt the only warmth in an
otherwise cold and lifeless climate.
name: The Eastern Spur

desc:
Even standing high on this peak of the mountains, you find yourself dwarfed
compared to the yet higher mountain top just north of you. Utter confusion fills
you when you look down. For some reason, the slope of the mountain looks
different. More and more, you feel completely lost in these mountains.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Even standing high on this peak of the mountains, you find yourself dwarfed
compared to the yet higher mountain top just north of you. Utter confusion fills
you when you look down. For some reason, the slope of the mountain looks
different. More and more, you feel completely lost in these mountains.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
High on this mountain top, you find that the air is very thin. So thin, that you
are unable to speak. Perhaps if the weather was ever good in this region, you
would have a wonderous view of the surrounding mountains. As it is, however, all
you can see are clouds, and more clouds.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
The rock underfoot is unstable, but you manage to keep your balance. The valley
in the east seems to roughly follow the line of peaks to the south-east, or you
can descend into the valley and then back up the side of another mountain.
name: On a Mountainside

desc:
Small breaks in the snow here allow you to descend the side of the mountain if
you wish, or you can continue along this ridge which leads to the south east. It
is difficult to see clearly, but you think there is a traversable valley in the
east.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The mountain drops down to a valley in the east, or you can travel along this
ridge to the south and west. On one of the highest peaks in this range, you can
see for miles around, even spotting a dark forest many miles to the north.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The mountains extend in a long sinuous chain to the north-west and south-east.
Far to the north you spot a dark line which could only be a path leading east
and west. Closer, you can see a valley in the east following this ridge of peaks
which you are travelling on.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
A small trail leads up the mountain to the east allowing to climb back up to the
ridge, or you can stay sheltered and travel west or south along the sides of
this large mountain, being careful not to stand on any loose scree which may
cause you to fall to your death hundreds of feet below.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
You can travel east or north, but you can see no way of climbing back up to the
top of the mountain from here. Rising majestically above you to both the north
and south, the snow covered peaks make you feel very small and insignificant in
comparison.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
You can travel east or north, but you can see no way of climbing back up to the
top of the mountain from here. Rising majestically above you to both the north
and south, the snow covered peaks make you feel very small and insignificant in
comparison.
name: On a Mountainside

desc:
You can either climb back up the mountain to the east, or travel west along the
mountainside. A large rock wall looms in the south, with a dark hole in the
middle of it probably indicating the entrance to a cave.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
The wind here chills you to the bone, and the rocks move beneath your feet.
Balancing precariously, you see that you can follow this ridge to the north and
east, or you can go into the shelter of the mountainside to the west.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
This cave has been worn from the rock by the passage of many millions of quarts
of water. This room is lit by the light streaming in from outside, but you can
see that the cave extending further back into the mountain is shrouded in
darkness. A foul odor avails your senses as you sniff around looking to see what
might be living here.
name: Cave Entrance

desc:
Shrouded in darkness the gloom here is depressing. Peering around you, you can
see that the cave extends to the west, or you can go back to the light in the
north where the entrance is.
name: Back of the Cave

desc:
A sulfurous stench comes from the water to the south, a lake which is wreathed
in mist. Peering into the gloom you can see the cave extending east and west,
and the ever present sound of dripping water un-nerves you in the darkness.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The constant drip..drip..drip of water puts your nerves on edge. The darkness
all around does nothing to allay any fears you may have about the inhabitants of
this cave complex, and neither does the powdered bones on the floor.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The light in here is eerie, and you see it comes from the luminous fungi growing
all over the walls and roof, in many shades of green, orange, red and yellow.
Obviously it is the decay of dead fungi that causes the awful stench in here. Or
is it... unidentifiable remains lie scattered about the floor.
name: Smelly Corner

desc:
A foul stench comes from the water to the south, and the steam rising from it
has a sulfurous smell. You spot a glimmer of surreal light from the north, but
to the east all you can see is the ever present, all pervading darkness.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Ahh, at last, some shelter from the wind. Although the sides of this mountain
are rather slippery, you don't have too much trouble keeping your footing. You
can travel further south, or climb back up to the mountain top.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
Mountains, mountains and more mountains. If they weren't so beautiful, I guess
you would be getting sick of them by now. Standing on the peak of this one, you
have an excellent view all around. You can follow this trail along the peaks
north or east. To the west you can descend a little to the side of the mountain
to get some shelter from the harsh winds.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The ridge of peaks along which you are walking extends further to the south and
west. You can descend the mountain to the east, but then you would not be able
to see these mountains in their full magnificence. You spot a valley in the east
following roughly the same direction as the ridge you are on.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The mist-shrouded water moves gently around you. Strange sounds are echoed and
amplified, distorted beyond recognition by the vast chamber in which this lake
sits. The water smells foul, the stench of sulfur and decay ever-present in your
poor nostrils. The lake extends to the south and west. You can also climb back
to the cave in the north.
name: Steaming Lake

desc:
You are gently rocked by ripples on the surface of this underground lake. Mist
and foul-smelling gases rise from the surface of the water, seemingly reaching
for your throat, trying to tear your lungs from your body. The lake extends to
the north. In the west you see a low island jutting above the water.
name: Steaming Lake

desc:
Ripples bounce backward and forward from the shore. The mist quickly rises up to
wrap itself around your throat, and you can almost feel it trying to strangle
you. The lake extends east, and to the south you spot a low island jutting above
the surface.
name: Steaming Lake

desc:
This rocky island must be where the inhabitants of the caves kept their most
precious possessions, for in the middle of the island sits a very heavy stone
chest with a very sturdy lock on it. The passage of many feet has worn the
surface of the rock smooth, and the island is just high enough to prevent the
mist covering it. Looking around, you see the lake to the east and north is
covered with mist, making you feel truly isolated here.
name: Rocky Island

desc:
The walls of this small chamber look like they have been carved from the rock by
crude tools. Natural stone archways lead to the north and south, probably a
continuation of the natural tunnel from which this chamber was hewn. To the east
there is a boulder which could possibly be moved.
name: Behind the Boulder

desc:
Above your head sits millions of tons of rock, just waiting for the right time
to come crashing down. The walls which arch up to the high stone ceiling are a
maze of hairline cracks. Bones in various states of decay litter the floor, but
most are so old that their origins are undeterminable.
name: Inside the Mountain

desc:
A large rockfall here has sealed off any progress further south, and the pile is
too large for you to climb across. However, on close inspection you see that
there is something not quite right about the way the rocks are arranged against
each other, and it looks like it may be possible to move the large rock on the
western side without causing the rest to crash down and bury you in debris.
name: Rockfall

desc:
Dark water swarms around you. The sound of rushing water floods the room. Cracks
and crevices along the edges of the cavern allow the water to slowly creep out
towards other underground tributaries.
name: A Pool

desc:
The skeletal remains of various creatures float aimlessly atop the water's
surface. Throughout the cave one can smell the stench of decay. Maggots cling to
the rocks feasting on the slowly eroding remains. At times bits of rotting flesh
form a layer of white debris lining the bottom of the water bed having been
stripped from the bones by what must have been the force of powerful water
creature.
name: Hidden Cave

desc:
This portion of the tunnels moves its way downward at a steady pace. Soon you
realise that you have descended a significant distance. The sounds of booted
feet echo around you, muffled in the damp air, giving you no clue as to their
general direction. Pools of water swarm into the room at an even current from
the south. Toward the northern section, the water level is as high as two or
three feet in depth. Dark blurs blink in and out of sight in the murky water.
Peering northward, the tunnels floor drops dramatically allowing the flowing
water to find a home.
name: Wet Tunnel

desc:
This natural cave looks rather old. The air here is damp, and the northern and
western walls bear no marks of any kind. To the east it looks as if somebody
has, rather brutally, battered the rock into a hole and then fitted a large iron
door there. To the south a small opening leads into a passage.
name: Small Cave

desc:
This small chamber is evidently a guarded entrance to an Orkish lair, perhaps to
the feared Goblin-town itself. A massive stone door lies to the south, hiding
contorted tunnels infested by unknown fell beasts and cunning, evil Orcs. The
walls are scarred with many blood-encrusted scratches, as if someone had used
them to clean a sword. A half-hidden crack leads to a small natural cave
connected to the outside, where Goblins can waylay hapless travellers. A sign
has been scribbled near the crack.
name: Orc Guard Room

desc:
As you have grown accustomed to, in these dull mountains, everything seems
bland. Everything, that is, except for the high peak just north of you. Although
you cannot seem to go either east nor west, you can follow the mountains both
north and south. In addition, you can climb a slope up to a mountain top.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A vast cave opens up inside the mountain, rising more than thirty feet tall.
Water trickles down the eastern wall into a small pond located centrally in the
cavern. Stalagmites and stalactites cover much of the cavern floor and ceiling.
Scattered about the cave lie crumbling bones and rotting pelts. A stone stair
has been carved out from the mountain, with a steep incline from the tunnel in
the west, to a tunnel entrance in the north. Small torches sit lit in iron claws
attached to the wall along the staircase.
name: Hollow in the Mountain

desc:
A long tunnel, more or less rounded, has been dug through the rock here. Sharp
edges of the cold, grey stone interrupt what could be a proper passageway. Along
the walls lie cracked pickaxes and abandoned broken ore carts. A thick layer of
dust covers the ground. Beams of dried, cracking wood support the tunnel,
keeping it from collapsing. A faint flickering light can be discerned at the end
of a passage that leads deeper down under the mountain.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The ground is rough and uneven. Footprints of various sizes and other
unidentified tracks run deep in the heavy layer of dust which covers the area.
Rocks lie heaped in big piles along the rounded tunnels. The ceiling and walls
are supported by thick, dark logs. A pile of boulders and rubble blocks the
passage to the east, while the darkness to the north hints at another cave or
tunnel in that direction. A tunnel slopes slightly downwards to the west.
name: Twisting and Turning Tunnel

desc:
The mines twist back and forth, with tunnels leading off in four directions.
Damp and dark, the excavation expands further to the east, south and west. A
hole in the northern wall leads into a steep, inclining tunnel. Cracked timber
shores the ceiling, keeping the tunnels from collapsing. Broken tools lie
abandoned in deep layers of dust along the tunnel's walls. A lantern hanging
from one of the beams in the ceiling emits a faint light.
name: Nexus

desc:
A wooden trough stands centrally placed in the damp cavern. An abundance of
pickaxes and heavy hammers are lined up in a rack on the northern wall. Wooden
beams steady the arched ceiling, keeping it from collapsing. Shackles connected
to heavy balls and chains lie grouped in different piles, just next to the
mining areas. The mine opens up to the west while a narrowing tunnel leads
deeper into the mountain to the south.
name: Excavation Site

desc:
An old mining tunnel comes to an end here, partially collapsed and completely in
ruins. From pebbles to boulders, broken planks to cracked wooden beams,
everything lie scattered about on the floor of the mine. The ceiling appears to
have caved in completely to the west. Humanoid bones poke out from the rubble,
crushed from the weight of the collapsed mine. The air is damp, and dust lies
heavily on the ground. A small passageway to north seems to be the only route
from this tunnel.
name: Collapsed Mine

desc:
A dwindling mineshaft descends deep into the mountain. Narrow, with rough walls
and ceiling, it twists its way downwards. The cavern itself is not very large,
and appears to have been dug out carelessly. Sharp edges protrude from the
walls; rocks and rubble lie scattered on the caverns floor, covered in a layer
of dust. The air is damp and heavy. There is a myriad of footprints, leading to
and fro between the mineshaft and the eastern wall.
name: Above a Mineshaft

desc:
Crates, chests and barrels crowd this small room. A table stands along the
southern wall with shards of clay and broken bottles scattered on it. An iron
shackle, nailed to the ceiling, holds a skeleton covered in ragged clothing. The
floor, walls and ceiling are smooth and heavy beams supports the ceiling,
tightly bound together by thick, black rope, creating a supporting arch. The
room exits through a door-opening in the east.
name: Storage Room

desc:
Several large boulders, surrounded by smaller rocks and shattered wooden beams,
create an upwards slope to the north. A charred skeleton has been crushed
underneath scorched timber. The cave was extensively mined in the past, but by
the looks of it has been abandoned for a long time. Carts have fallen over,
broken into many pieces. Some of their load has a small shine to it, beneath the
covering dust.
name: Collapsed Mineshaft

desc:
This is a simply constructed cabin, yet it looks solid enough to withstand
weather and wind. There is one window each on the northern and southern walls. A
door to the west seems to be the cabin's only exit aside from a trapdoor of
solid-looking wood in the floor. An old rug lies on the floor in the centre of
the room, covered in dust and dirt. An old burnt-out lantern is standing on a
small table next to a bed. The bed itself is tipped over, and its mattress and
cover lie discarded in a corner, gathering dust and mould.
name: An Abandoned Mountain Cabin

desc:
There is a small cabinet in one of the corners. A small wooden table has been
placed between two shelves. Some cutlery and old, dirty plates lie covered in
dust on the floor. Mouldy groceries have been left to rot on the shelves. The
tight space opens into a main room to the south.
name: Storage Room

desc:
The cellar is in complete disarray. Bookshelves cover the walls on all sides.
The books, pamphlets, scrolls and parchments that once stood on said shelves lie
scattered on the floor. An old lantern lies in pieces on the ground. Another
lantern, unlit and its glass cracked, hangs from the ceiling. Several simple
chests stand along the walls, their locks broken or lids smashed, and their
contents apparently looted. A small, unsteady staircase leads up to another room
above.
name: Underground Study

desc:
The shaft is completely flooded with with glacial water; lumps of ice float
lazily to the surface. You can climb your way up or down along the ladder, but
you cannot survive this shivering cold for long. Below you the tunnel is filled
in with great boulders.
name: A Flooded Mineshaft

desc:
You are at the end of a wide road leading into a spacious cavern. The road is
pocked by large craters and widening cracks, but still you can see the care and
skill with which it was formed. To the west a stately cavern gracefully opens to
receive the road. To the east the road ends abruptly at a sheer cliff, the work
of some avalanche or giant's rage in ages long since passed.
name: Entrance to a Cave
